name: LangChain
commonFailureModes: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
Tool call loops in agent executors
Context window overflow in long conversations
Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
integrationMethod: Wrap chain.invoke() with vex.verify()
description: The most popular framework for building LLM-powered applications with chains, agents, and retrieval.

name: CrewAI
commonFailureModes: Agent delegation loops between crew members
Hallucinated tool outputs passed between agents
Task drift when agents reinterpret objectives
Inconsistent output quality across crew roles
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a step callback on each agent task
description: Multi-agent orchestration framework where autonomous AI agents collaborate on complex tasks.

name: AutoGen
commonFailureModes: Infinite conversation loops between agents
Code execution producing unsafe outputs
Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
integrationMethod: Register Vex as a reply function filter
description: Microsoft's framework for building multi-agent conversational AI systems with human-in-the-loop.

name: LlamaIndex
commonFailureModes: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
Index staleness causing outdated responses
Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
integrationMethod: Wrap query_engine.query() with vex.verify()
description: Data framework for connecting LLMs with external data sources via indexing and retrieval.

name: Vercel AI SDK
commonFailureModes: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
Tool call results not validated before display
Inconsistent structured output across providers
Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
integrationMethod: Add Vex middleware to generateText/streamText
description: Vercel's TypeScript toolkit for building AI-powered streaming UIs with React Server Components.

name: OpenAI Agents SDK
commonFailureModes: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
Tool output not validated before agent consumption
Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
integrationMethod: Add Vex as an output guardrail on each agent
description: OpenAI's official SDK for building production multi-agent systems with handoffs, guardrails, and tracing.

name: Semantic Kernel
commonFailureModes: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
Planner generating invalid execution plans
Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
Cross-plugin data contamination
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a kernel filter on function results
description: Microsoft's enterprise SDK for integrating LLMs into applications with plugins, planners, and memory.

name: Haystack
commonFailureModes: Pipeline component output type mismatches
Retriever returning irrelevant documents
Generator hallucinating beyond retrieved context
Custom component errors silently swallowed
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a custom component in the pipeline
description: Deepset's framework for building production-ready LLM applications with composable pipelines.

name: AG2
commonFailureModes: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
Code execution agents producing harmful outputs
Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
integrationMethod: Register Vex as a message hook on agents
description: Community fork of AutoGen focused on multi-agent conversations with enhanced group chat capabilities.

name: Smolagents
commonFailureModes: Code agent generating unsafe Python execution
Tool agent misinterpreting tool descriptions
Multi-step reasoning errors compounding
Minimal error handling in generated code
integrationMethod: Wrap agent.run() with vex.verify()
description: Hugging Face's lightweight agent framework emphasizing simplicity and code-based reasoning.

name: Google ADK
commonFailureModes: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
Session state corruption in long conversations
Tool response format mismatches across agents
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a before_agent_callback
description: Google's Agent Development Kit for building multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models.

name: DSPy
commonFailureModes: Optimized prompts overfitting to training examples
Chain-of-thought reasoning producing plausible but wrong logic
Assertion violations not caught at inference time
Module composition creating unexpected output distributions
integrationMethod: Wrap module forward() calls with vex.verify()
description: Stanford's framework for programming (not prompting) LLMs with composable, optimizable modules.

name: Instructor
commonFailureModes: Structured output validation retries exhausted
Partial extraction missing required fields
Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
integrationMethod: Wrap client.chat.completions.create() with vex.verify()
description: Structured output extraction library that patches LLM clients to return validated Pydantic models.

name: Mastra
commonFailureModes: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
Agent tool calls returning malformed data
Memory retrieval injecting stale context
Sync integration data causing agent confusion
integrationMethod: Add Vex as middleware in the Mastra agent pipeline
description: TypeScript framework for building AI agents with workflows, knowledge, and third-party integrations.

name: BeeAI
commonFailureModes: Agent memory growing unbounded in long sessions
Tool validation gaps allowing injection
Serialization errors in agent state persistence
Granite model output format inconsistencies
integrationMethod: Register Vex as an event listener on agent events
description: IBM's open-source framework for building production AI agents with memory, tools, and observability.

name: LangGraph
commonFailureModes: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
integrationMethod: Wrap graph.invoke() with vex.verify() or add Vex as a graph node
description: LangChain's stateful multi-actor agent framework for building complex, cyclical agent workflows as graphs.

name: OpenAI Assistants API
commonFailureModes: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
integrationMethod: Add Vex verification as a webhook on run completion events
description: OpenAI's managed agent runtime with built-in code interpreter, file search, and function calling capabilities.

name: Amazon Bedrock Agents
commonFailureModes: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
integrationMethod: Wrap invoke_agent() responses with vex.verify() post-processing
description: AWS fully managed agent service with knowledge bases, action groups, and guardrails for enterprise deployments.

name: Azure AI Agent Service
commonFailureModes: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
integrationMethod: Add Vex as middleware in the Azure AI agent processing pipeline
description: Microsoft's enterprise agent platform with Azure OpenAI integration, grounding, and enterprise security controls.

name: Agno
commonFailureModes: Multi-modal agent misinterpreting image or audio inputs
Agentic session memory recalling fabricated past interactions
Structured output failing validation on complex nested schemas
Team agent coordination producing contradictory sub-results
integrationMethod: Wrap agent.run() with vex.verify() decorator
description: Lightweight model-agnostic agent framework for building multi-modal AI agents, formerly known as Phidata.

name: n8n AI Agents
commonFailureModes: Workflow trigger chains causing recursive agent execution
AI node outputs not validated before passing to action nodes
Credential exposure through agent tool call parameters
Memory window nodes injecting stale context into conversations
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a webhook node after AI agent nodes in the workflow
description: Open-source workflow automation platform with native AI agent nodes for building complex agentic workflows.

name: Flowise
commonFailureModes: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a callback handler in the Flowise chatflow configuration
description: Low-code LLM orchestration platform for building customized AI agents and RAG pipelines via drag-and-drop.

name: Llama Stack
commonFailureModes: Safety shield filtering producing false positives on benign content
Agentic loop turn limits exceeded without meaningful resolution
Tool runtime responses not conforming to expected schema
Memory bank retrieval hallucinating document associations
integrationMethod: Wrap agent turn responses with vex.verify() post-processing
description: Meta's standardized interface for building Llama-powered agents with safety, memory, and tool use capabilities.

name: Pydantic AI
commonFailureModes: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
integrationMethod: Add Vex as a result_validator on agent definitions
description: Type-safe AI agent framework by the Pydantic team, designed for production applications with structured outputs.

name: Claude Tool Use
commonFailureModes: Tool use chains producing cascading errors from early tool failures
Parallel tool calls returning conflicting results without resolution
Extended thinking producing reasoning inconsistent with final output
Computer use agent performing unintended actions on screen elements
integrationMethod: Wrap messages.create() tool_use responses with vex.verify()
description: Anthropic's native tool use and agentic capabilities for building reliable AI agents with Claude models.
